questionGroups.instruction: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.prompt: What does the notice say?

options.label: Garden waste will not be collected next week.
Residents must bring waste to the town hall.

questions.explanation: Thông báo tạm ngừng thu gom rác vườn do đào tạo nhân viên.

questions.prompt: What does Mum remind Rachel about?
questions.explanation: Mẹ nhắc Rachel mang đồ thể dục (PE kit).

questions.prompt: What is true about the classes?

options.label: They are for people who speak little English.

questions.explanation: Lớp cho người mới bắt đầu (beginners) – ít tiếng Anh.

questions.prompt: What should visitors bring?
questions.explanation: Yêu cầu mang túi riêng để đựng sách đã mua.

questions.prompt: Why is school closing early?
questions.explanation: Do đợt nóng (heatwave).

questions.prompt: What should you do if you find the phone?
questions.explanation: Liên hệ Tom theo số điện thoại đã cho.

parts.passageSubtitle: Young business owners share their stories.

passage.label: Nina
passage.text: I started my own organic skincare business when I was 17. I had sensitive skin myself and couldn't find any products that worked for me without harsh chemicals. So I began making my own creams and soaps in my kitchen. I gave samples to my friends and they all loved them. I then started selling them online and at local farmers' markets. Now, three years later, I have a small team of employees and my products are sold in 20 shops across the country. I'm proud that all my ingredients are natural and sustainably sourced. My next goal is to expand to other countries.

passage.label: Oscar
passage.text: My business is an app that helps people find the cheapest petrol prices in their area. I created it when I was 16 because I noticed my parents were always complaining about expensive fuel. I taught myself to code and spent months building the app. It took a while to attract users, but now over 10,000 people use it every day. I earn money from advertising and I also offer a premium version without ads for a small monthly fee. I'm currently studying computer science at university, but I still manage the app in my free time. I hope to create more useful apps in the future.

passage.label: Maya
passage.text: I've always loved photography, so I started a photography service for events like weddings and birthdays. I was 15 when I took photos at my cousin's wedding – everyone loved the pictures and asked me to take more. I bought professional equipment with my savings and started advertising online. Now, I have a full schedule every weekend. I also teach photography classes to beginners at the community centre. My business has given me the freedom to be creative and meet new people. I'm thinking of opening a photography studio next year.

questionGroups.instruction: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.prompt: Who started a business because they had a personal problem?
questions.explanation: Nina có làn da nhạy cảm và không tìm được sản phẩm phù hợp.

questions.prompt: Who taught themselves a technical skill to start their business?
questions.explanation: Oscar tự học lập trình để tạo ứng dụng.

questions.prompt: Who now helps others learn their skill?
questions.explanation: Maya dạy nhiếp ảnh cho người mới bắt đầu.

questions.prompt: Who sells products that are good for the environment?
questions.explanation: Nina dùng nguyên liệu tự nhiên và bền vững.

questions.prompt: Who has a business that helps people save money?
questions.explanation: Ứng dụng của Oscar giúp tìm giá xăng rẻ nhất.

questions.prompt: Who started their business because people encouraged them?
questions.explanation: Maya được mọi người khen và yêu cầu chụp thêm.

questions.prompt: Who plans to grow their business internationally?
questions.explanation: Nina muốn mở rộng sang các nước khác.

parts.passageSubtitle: How technology is changing the way we drive.

passage.text: Electric cars have become increasingly popular in recent years. Many people are choosing them because they are cheaper to run than traditional petrol cars and produce no emissions. However, the technology is still developing, and there are some challenges that need to be overcome.

One major issue is the limited range of electric cars on a single charge. Most models can travel between 200 and 400 kilometres before needing to be recharged. This can be inconvenient for long journeys, especially in areas where charging stations are rare. To solve this, governments and companies are investing heavily in building more charging infrastructure.

Another problem is the cost. Although electric cars are cheaper to run, they are often more expensive to buy than petrol cars. The main reason is the battery, which is very expensive to produce. However, prices have been falling as technology improves, and many experts predict that electric cars will become more affordable within the next five years.

The environmental benefits of electric cars are clear, but there are also hidden costs. For example, producing the batteries requires mining for rare metals, which can damage the environment. Moreover, if the electricity used to charge the cars comes from coal-fired power stations, they are not completely clean. Nevertheless, they are still a better option than petrol cars overall.

Many countries have announced plans to ban the sale of new petrol and diesel cars by 2030 or 2035. This has encouraged car manufacturers to accelerate their electric vehicle production. The future of transport is likely to be electric, and we are only at the beginning of this revolution.

questionGroups.instruction: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.prompt: Why are electric cars becoming popular?

options.label: They are faster than petrol cars.
They cost less to operate and are eco-friendly.
They have a very long range.

questions.explanation: Chạy rẻ hơn và không thải khí.

questions.prompt: What is the first challenge mentioned for electric cars?

options.label: The shortage of charging stations for long trips.

questions.explanation: Tầm đường ngắn và thiếu trạm sạc.

questions.prompt: Why are electric cars more expensive to buy?

options.label: Because they are imported from abroad.
Because the battery is costly to make.

questions.explanation: Pin rất đắt để sản xuất.

questions.prompt: What hidden environmental cost is mentioned?

options.label: Mining for metals to make batteries.

questions.explanation: Khai thác kim loại hiếm cho pin gây hại môi trường.

questions.prompt: What is the main point of the final paragraph?

options.label: Electric cars will soon replace petrol cars.
Governments are banning all cars by 2030.
Manufacturers are not interested in electric cars.

questions.explanation: Nhiều nước ban hành lệnh cấm xe xăng, và tương lai là xe điện.

passage.text: Being a firefighter is one of the most dangerous but rewarding jobs. Firefighters are (19) ________ to respond to emergencies such as fires, car accidents, and floods. They must be physically fit and (20) ________ under pressure.

Before becoming a firefighter, candidates have to pass a series of (21) ________ tests, including written exams and physical fitness assessments. They also (22) ________ extensive training in using equipment and rescuing people.

Firefighters work in shifts, often (23) ________ long hours. They have to be ready at all times. Despite the risks, many firefighters say they love their job (24) ________ they can save lives and help their community.

questionGroups.instruction: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.prompt: Firefighters are (19) ________ to respond to emergencies.
questions.explanation: Được huấn luyện (trained) để ứng phó.

questions.prompt: They must be physically fit and (20) ________ under pressure.
questions.explanation: Giữ bình tĩnh (stay calm) dưới áp lực.

questions.prompt: Candidates have to pass a series of (21) ________ tests.
questions.explanation: Nhiều bài kiểm tra khác nhau (different).

questions.prompt: They also (22) ________ extensive training in using equipment.
questions.explanation: Nhận (receive) đào tạo chuyên sâu.

questions.prompt: Firefighters work in shifts, often (23) ________ long hours.
questions.explanation: Làm việc (working) nhiều giờ.

questions.prompt: Many firefighters say they love their job (24) ________ they can save lives.
questions.explanation: Bởi vì (because) họ có thể cứu người.

passage.text:
Dear Maria,
Thank you for your letter. I was so happy to hear from (0) ________. I'm sorry I haven't written for a while, but I've been really busy with school.

We are doing exams at the moment. I have to study every day, (25) ________ I don't get much free time. But next week everything will be over and I (26) ________ have a holiday. I'm going to visit my grandparents in the countryside.

They live (27) ________ a small village near the mountains. I love going there because it's so peaceful. I can go for long walks and breathe fresh air. My grandfather has a vegetable garden, and he always gives (28) ________ some fresh tomatoes and beans to take home.

(29) ________ you doing anything exciting this summer? Maybe you can come and visit me one day. We could go to the beach together. Write (30) ________ soon and tell me your news.

Best wishes,
Anna

questionGroups.instruction: For each question, write the correct answer. Write ONE word for each gap.

questions.prompt: Write ONE word.
questions.explanation: so = vì vậy (kết quả).

questions.prompt: Write ONE word.
questions.explanation: will have = sẽ có (tương lai).

questions.prompt: Write ONE word.
questions.explanation: in a village = trong làng.

questions.prompt: Write ONE word.
questions.explanation: give me = cho tôi.

questions.prompt: Write ONE word.
questions.explanation: Are you doing...? (câu hỏi thì hiện tại tiếp diễn).

questions.prompt: Write ONE word.
questions.explanation: write back = viết thư trả lời.
